key_highlights: Stocks rise on AI optimism and oil climbs amid stalled Iran deal negotiations, with Wall Street risk rally continuing despite geopolitical tensions.
Berkshire Hathaway dumps entire stake in a dividend stock and announces $6.8 billion acquisition of Taylor Morrison, signaling strategic portfolio shift.
Oil records biggest one-month drop in six years, but prices rise from six-week low as Iran deal remains elusive, creating two-sided risk.
SpaceX cuts valuation to $1.8 trillion but remains on track for world's largest IPO, forcing Wall Street reorganization.
SoftBank pledges €75 billion to build Europe's biggest AI facility in France, underscoring massive AI infrastructure investment.
Nvidia's latest product is a game-changer, while Renaissance Technologies opens new position in Apple and trims Palantir.
IMF chief holds talks with Venezuelan officials on economy issues, and ECB signals sooner rather than later action.
China's factory activity beats forecasts in May, luxury spending rebounds, but investors turn sellers of HK stocks in rare reversal.
Ebola outbreak in Congo widens, adding to global health concerns alongside Ukraine drone warfare redefining battlefield dynamics.
Jim Cramer highlights buying opportunities in Walmart, Lowe's, and Realty Income, while expressing caution on ServiceNow and Bentley Systems.

sectors.trend: Strong AI-driven momentum with massive infrastructure investments (SoftBank €75bn, Nvidia product launch), but selective profit-taking by smart money (Renaissance trimming Palantir, opening Apple).
sectors.implications: AI remains the dominant theme; focus on companies with direct AI exposure and strong fundamentals. Watch for valuation pullbacks in overhyped names.

sectors.trend: Oil prices volatile: biggest monthly drop in six years but rising from six-week low as Iran deal remains elusive. Goldman sees two-sided risk from lower use offsetting Iran war premium.
sectors.implications: Energy sector offers tactical trading opportunities but high uncertainty. Petrobras cuts diesel prices amid subsidy plan. Long-term demand concerns persist.

sectors.trend: Positive trial results (EFS with selpercatinib in RET+ NSCLC), Revolution Medicines shipping pancreatic cancer drug, and Renaissance liking United Therapeutics. Ebola outbreak in Congo adds urgency.
sectors.implications: Biotech offers selective opportunities in oncology and rare diseases. Ebola outbreak may boost vaccine/therapeutics stocks. Regulatory and trial risks remain.

sectors.trend: Mixed: China luxury spending rebounds, but US new car market loses 1 million buyers. Jim Cramer sees bottom in Mattel, strong merchandising at Ralph Lauren, and off-price strength at TJX.
sectors.implications: Selective opportunities in luxury and off-price retail. Auto sector faces structural headwinds. Home improvement (Lowe's, Home Depot) beating expectations.

sectors.trend: Jim Cramer bullish on Realty Income (O) expecting higher prices. Berkshire acquires Taylor Morrison for $6.8B, signaling confidence in housing. Pool Corp needs more housing turnover.
sectors.implications: REITs and homebuilders benefit from rate stabilization and housing demand. Berkshire's move validates sector. Monitor interest rate sensitivity.

sectors.trend: Bond trader bets on Fed hike from jobs data. ECB signals sooner action. India rate risks and fiscal worries pressure bond yields. Private equity involved in Evoke takeover financing.
sectors.implications: Financials face mixed signals: rate hikes benefit banks but hurt bond prices. M&A activity (Fibra MTY/Fibra Macquarie, Ryan/Svalner) shows deal flow. Watch central bank moves.

sectors.trend: SpaceX IPO forces Wall Street reorganization, valuation cut to $1.8T but still world's largest. Blue Origin explosion makes NASA dependent on SpaceX. Ukraine drone warfare redefines dynamics.
sectors.implications: Space and defense are high-growth areas. SpaceX IPO is a major event. Drone warfare and geopolitical tensions support defense spending. Monitor regulatory and technical risks.

sectors.trend: Multiple headlines highlight Beyond Meat, Steakholder Foods, Tyson Foods, Pluri, US Foods, Sysco, Performance Food Group as elite or prominent alternative meat stocks.
sectors.implications: Sector gaining investor attention but still speculative. Regulatory approvals and consumer adoption key. Diversified food companies (Tyson, Sysco) offer lower risk exposure.

key_themes: AI infrastructure boom (SoftBank, Nvidia, Palantir, ServiceNow)
Geopolitical uncertainty (Iran deal, Ukraine, Venezuela, Ebola)
Central bank divergence (Fed hike bets, ECB sooner action, India rate risks)
M&A activity (Berkshire/Taylor Morrison, Fibra MTY/Macquarie, Ryan/Svalner, Evoke)
Space race and IPO (SpaceX, Blue Origin, NASA dependency)
Consumer resilience vs. weakness (luxury rebound, auto slump, grocery hacks)
Energy volatility (oil price drop, Iran talks, demand concerns)
Healthcare innovation (cancer trials, pancreatic drug, Ebola response)
Tax optimization strategies (dividend stacking, estate planning)
Crypto and digital assets (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, XRP, Dogecoin, corporate treasury shutdowns)

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across regions and sectors; hold defensive assets (gold, treasuries); monitor diplomatic developments closely.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Central bank policy misstep (Fed hike, ECB action, India rates)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain duration-neutral bond positioning; favor floating rate instruments; hedge against rate volatility with options.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil price volatility and demand uncertainty
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce direct energy exposure; use commodity ETFs for tactical trades; focus on companies with hedged production.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI valuation bubble and concentration risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on high-flying AI names; rotate to value and defensive sectors; use equal-weight indices.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Global health crisis (Ebola outbreak in Congo)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor WHO updates; consider vaccine/therapeutics stocks for hedging; avoid travel to affected regions.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer spending slowdown (auto market, grocery hacks, Gen Z trends)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on defensive consumer staples and discount retailers; avoid auto and luxury if weakness persists.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and legal risks (AI copyright, Meta tracking, Honda recall, DirecTV changes)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across sectors; avoid single-stock concentration in companies facing regulatory scrutiny.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Corporate treasury crypto exposure (another company shuts down Bitcoin treasury)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Limit crypto allocation to speculative portion of portfolio; prefer regulated crypto ETFs.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to AI infrastructure beneficiaries (Nvidia, SoftBank-backed ventures, Palantir on pullbacks)
opportunities.rationale: Massive capital flows into AI (SoftBank €75bn, Nvidia product launch) and strong demand for AI services. Renaissance trimming Palantir may create entry points.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy homebuilders and REITs on Berkshire's Taylor Morrison acquisition and Cramer's bullish Realty Income call
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire's $6.8B acquisition signals confidence in housing. Lowe's and Home Depot beating expectations. Realty Income offers income and growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Walmart on pullbacks as a rare buying opportunity per Cramer
opportunities.rationale: Walmart's defensive nature, strong quarter, and consumer staple positioning make it attractive during uncertainty. Cramer sees pullback as opportunity.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider selective biotech plays (Revolution Medicines, United Therapeutics, Ardelyx)
opportunities.rationale: Positive trial results and new drug shipments (pancreatic cancer) indicate innovation. Renaissance likes UTHR. Ebola outbreak may boost vaccine stocks.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in SpaceX via pre-IPO funds or wait for IPO
opportunities.rationale: World's largest IPO at $1.8T valuation, forcing Wall Street reorganization. Strong growth in space and defense. Blue Origin setback makes SpaceX dominant.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Chinese luxury and consumer stocks on rebound
opportunities.rationale: China factory activity beats forecasts, luxury spending rebounds, and stock market recovery supports consumer confidence. TSMC valuation gap narrowing.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce energy exposure and hedge with gold (Kinross Gold)
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil volatility high with Iran deal uncertainty and demand concerns. Goldman sees two-sided risk. Gold offers safe haven. Renaissance likes KGC.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to defensive consumer staples (Costco, TJX, Sysco)
defensive_moves.rationale: Costco strong quarter but valuation high; TJX off-price strength; Sysco alternative meat exposure. Defensive during potential slowdown.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hold cash or short-term treasuries ahead of Fed decision
defensive_moves.rationale: Bond trader bets on Fed hike from jobs data. Rate uncertainty warrants caution. Short-term treasuries offer yield with low duration risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify into tax-advantaged strategies (Roth dividend stacking)
defensive_moves.rationale: Tax-aware dividend stacking can generate $42k federally tax-free on $750k Roth portfolio. Estate planning can save $700k tax gap.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid overvalued AI names and take profits on high flyers
defensive_moves.rationale: Cramer cautious on ServiceNow, Bentley Systems. Renaissance trimming Palantir. Valuation concerns in Snowflake. Rotate to value.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets likely to remain volatile with a positive bias driven by AI momentum and M&A activity, but geopolitical risks (Iran, Ukraine, Venezuela) and central bank uncertainty (Fed hike, ECB action) could trigger pullbacks. Oil may stabilize but demand concerns persist. Consumer sector shows mixed signals. Focus on jobs data and Iran deal developments.
market_outlook.long_term: Structural growth in AI, space, and biotech offers long-term opportunities. Housing and consumer staples provide defensive growth. Geopolitical tensions and health crises (Ebola) are wildcards. Central bank normalization will be gradual. China's recovery and luxury rebound support global demand. SpaceX IPO and SoftBank AI facility are transformative events.
key_catalysts: US jobs data (June 2026) - potential trigger for Fed rate hike
Iran nuclear deal outcome - impacts oil prices and geopolitical risk
ECB policy meeting - sooner action signal
Nvidia product launch impact on AI sector
China economic data (factory activity, luxury sales)
Berkshire Hathaway portfolio moves (Taylor Morrison acquisition, dividend stock sale)
Ukraine conflict developments and drone warfare evolution
watch_list: Fed funds futures and bond market pricing of rate hikes
Oil inventory data and Iran deal headlines
AI company earnings and guidance (Nvidia, Palantir, ServiceNow)
Homebuilder and REIT performance post-Berkshire acquisition
Biotech trial results and FDA approvals
Consumer spending data (retail sales, auto sales)
Crypto market trends (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, Dogecoin)
SpaceX IPO filing and roadshow updates
Ebola case counts and WHO alerts
China-HK stock flows and valuation gap
Tax policy changes affecting dividend stacking and estate planning
